name: Mobile and Systems Frameworks

tasks.title: Write Kotlin/Android class prompt
tasks.body: Author internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/kotlin/android.md (30-60 lines). Cover: Android patterns (Activity/Fragment lifecycle, Jetpack Compose UI, ViewModel with StateFlow/SharedFlow, Hilt dependency injection), Navigation component, Room database, Retrofit/Ktor for networking, coroutines and structured concurrency in Android (viewModelScope, lifecycleScope), Gradle build configuration, testing with JUnit (Robolectric for unit tests, Compose testing rules, Espresso for instrumented tests, Turbine for Flow testing), and common pitfalls (lifecycle-unaware coroutine launches leaking, configuration change state loss, Compose recomposition performance from unstable types, deep Activity back stack bugs, ProGuard/R8 stripping needed classes). Do NOT repeat Kotlin language fundamentals. Use 'prefer X' phrasing. Research current Jetpack Compose / Android 14+ patterns. Zero wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Write Swift/iOS class prompt
tasks.body: Author internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/swift/ios.md (30-60 lines). Cover: iOS/SwiftUI patterns (declarative views, @State/@Binding/@ObservedObject/@EnvironmentObject, MVVM with ObservableObject), UIKit interop (UIViewRepresentable, UIViewControllerRepresentable), navigation (NavigationStack, NavigationPath), Core Data / SwiftData, async/await with structured concurrency in iOS (MainActor, Task groups), SPM dependency management, testing with XCTest (UI testing, ViewInspector for SwiftUI, mock protocols, async test expectations), and common pitfalls (retain cycles from strong self in closures, main thread violations in UI updates, SwiftUI view identity and performance from excessive body recomputation, Core Data context threading violations, missing @MainActor annotations). Do NOT repeat Swift language fundamentals. Use 'prefer X' phrasing. Research current SwiftUI / iOS 17+ patterns. Zero wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Write Dart/Flutter class prompt
tasks.body: Author internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/dart/flutter.md (30-60 lines). Cover: Flutter patterns (widget tree composition, StatelessWidget vs StatefulWidget, state management with Riverpod or Bloc/Cubit), navigation (GoRouter, Navigator 2.0), platform channels for native interop, theming and Material/Cupertino design, build flavors and environments, testing with flutter_test (widget tests with WidgetTester, pumpWidget, golden tests, integration tests with patrol or integration_test, mockito for service mocks), and common pitfalls (setState after dispose, unbounded ListView children in Column, key misuse causing state loss, platform-specific rendering differences, deep widget nesting harming readability). Do NOT repeat Dart language fundamentals. Use 'prefer X' phrasing. Research current Flutter 3.x patterns. Zero wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Write Rust/Actix class prompt
tasks.body: Author internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/rust/actix.md (30-60 lines). Cover: Actix Web patterns (App builder, resource routing, extractors for Path/Query/Json/Data, middleware with Transform/Service), application state with web::Data and Arc, error handling (ResponseError trait, custom error types), database integration (sqlx or Diesel with actix_web::web::block for sync), WebSocket actors, testing with actix_web::test (TestRequest, init_service, call_service, TestServer for integration), and common pitfalls (move semantics with closures in route handlers, Send + Sync requirements for shared state, blocking the async runtime with synchronous database drivers, confusing actix actor system with actix-web, payload size limits). Do NOT repeat Rust language fundamentals. Use 'prefer X' phrasing. Research current Actix Web 4.x patterns. Zero wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Write Rust/Tokio class prompt
tasks.body: Author internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/rust/tokio.md (30-60 lines). Cover: Tokio patterns (runtime configuration, spawning tasks, JoinHandle management, select\! macro, channels: mpsc/oneshot/broadcast/watch), structured concurrency with JoinSet and TaskTracker, async I/O (TcpListener/TcpStream, tokio::fs, BufReader/BufWriter), synchronization primitives (tokio::sync::Mutex vs std::sync::Mutex, RwLock, Semaphore, Notify), graceful shutdown patterns, testing async code (tokio::test macro, time::pause for deterministic timing, mock I/O), and common pitfalls (holding a std::sync::Mutex across an .await, spawning without storing JoinHandle losing panics, blocking the runtime with CPU-bound work instead of spawn_blocking, select\! branch cancellation semantics, unbounded channel memory growth). Do NOT repeat Rust language fundamentals. Use 'prefer X' phrasing. Research current Tokio 1.x patterns. Zero wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Mobile and Systems Frameworks is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify that kotlin/android, swift/ios, dart/flutter, rust/actix, and rust/tokio prompt files each exist, are 30-60 lines, use 'prefer X' phrasing, contain no parent-language fundamentals, and contain no wolfcastle references

breadcrumbs.text: Add Kotlin/Android class prompt covering Compose, Hilt, lifecycle coroutines, Room, Retrofit, Gradle config, testing, and common pitfalls [task-0001]
Add Swift/iOS class prompt covering SwiftUI, MVVM, NavigationStack, UIKit interop, SwiftData/Core Data, structured concurrency, SPM, testing, and common pitfalls [task-0002]

Add Dart/Flutter class prompt covering widget architecture, Riverpod/Bloc state management, go_router navigation, platform channels, Material 3 theming, build flavors, testing with flutter_test/integration_test/patrol, and common pitfalls [task-0003]

task-0001.objective: Author the Kotlin/Android framework class prompt for coding tasks
task-0001.what_happened: Created internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/kotlin/android.md (45 lines). Studied the existing kotlin.md language prompt, go.md, python/fastapi.md, and java/spring.md for format and depth calibration. Wrote sections covering Compose UI with unidirectional data flow, Hilt DI, lifecycle-aware coroutines, Room/Retrofit data layer, Gradle configuration with version catalogs, testing (Robolectric, Compose testing, Espresso, Turbine), and four pitfall entries. Avoided repeating Kotlin fundamentals already in kotlin.md.
went_well: Clean separation from the language-level kotlin.md; no overlap on coroutine basics, scope functions, or null safety
Pitfalls section covers the specific Android failure modes requested: lifecycle-unaware launches, config change state loss, Compose recomposition from unstable types, and ProGuard stripping
improvements: Could add a section on WorkManager patterns and predictive back gesture API once those stabilize further

task-0002.objective: Author the Swift/iOS framework class prompt for coding tasks
task-0002.what_happened: Created internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/swift/ios.md (39 lines). Studied the existing swift.md language prompt plus kotlin/android.md, python/fastapi.md, and java/spring.md for format and depth calibration. Wrote sections covering SwiftUI with MVVM and @Observable, NavigationStack routing, UIKit interop via Representable protocols, SwiftData and Core Data persistence, structured concurrency with @MainActor and TaskGroup, SPM dependency management, testing with XCTest/ViewInspector/XCUIApplication, and four pitfall entries. Carefully avoided repeating Swift fundamentals already in swift.md (value types, guard, protocol-oriented design, async/await basics, retain cycles in general, force unwrapping, IUOs, ABI stability).
went_well: Clean separation from swift.md; no overlap on language fundamentals, only iOS-specific patterns
Covers the iOS 17+ @Observable migration path alongside ObservableObject for older targets
Pitfalls section addresses the specific failure modes requested: retain cycles in iOS context, main thread violations, SwiftUI view identity and recomposition, and Core Data context threading
improvements: Could expand UIKit interop section with more detail on Coordinator patterns for complex delegates once more adoption data is available

task-0003.objective: Author the Dart/Flutter framework class prompt for coding tasks
task-0003.what_happened: Created internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/dart/flutter.md (47 lines). Studied the existing dart.md language prompt, kotlin/android.md, and swift/ios.md for format and depth calibration. Wrote sections covering widget architecture and composition, state management (Riverpod and Bloc/Cubit), go_router navigation, platform channels and Pigeon, Material 3 theming with ColorScheme.fromSeed, build flavors with dart-define, testing (flutter_test, integration_test, patrol, mocktail), and four pitfall entries. Carefully avoided repeating Dart fundamentals already in dart.md (null safety, late, BuildContext async gap, Future error swallowing, collection equality, pubspec constraints).
went_well: Clean separation from dart.md; no overlap on language fundamentals, only Flutter-specific patterns
Covers both Riverpod 2.x and Bloc/Cubit patterns with guidance on when to use each
Pitfalls section addresses the specific failure modes requested: setState after dispose, unbounded ListView in Column, key misuse, platform rendering differences
improvements: Could expand the platform channels section with more detail on EventChannel error handling once patterns stabilize
